name: sweetpad
description: Develop Swift/iOS projects in VS Code

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run build:sweetpad-lib:release && cross-env NODE_ENV=production rolldown -c rolldown.config.mjs
scripts.check:format: oxfmt src scripts docs rolldown.config.mjs vitest.config.ts
scripts.check:all: npm run check:format && npm run check:lint && npm run check:types

workspace.description: Path to workspace for resolving dependencies

simulator.description: Simulator to launch. Currently only device UUID is supported. Deprecated: use `destinationId` instead

destinationId.description: Device ID to launch the app on

destination.description: Raw destination string to launch the app on. Alternative to `destinationId`

launchArgs.description: Arguments to pass to the app on launch

launchEnv.description: Environment variables to pass to the app on launch

configurationSnippets.label: SweetPad: Build and Run (Wait for debugger)

body.name: SweetPad: Build and Run (Wait for debugger)

codelldbAttributes.title: CodeLLDB attributes
codelldbAttributes.description: Attributes to pass to CodeLLDB. See: https://github.com/vadimcn/codelldb/blob/v1.10.0/MANUAL.md

continueOnAttach.description: Continue the app after attaching. If false, the app will be paused after attaching.

codelldbAttributes.title: CodeLLDB attributes
codelldbAttributes.description: Attributes to pass to CodeLLDB. See: https://github.com/vadimcn/codelldb/blob/v1.10.0/MANUAL.md

viewsWelcome.contents:
No Xcode scheme was found. Select the workspace that has the Xcode project to use SweetPad.
[Select Workspace](command:sweetpad.build.selectXcodeWorkspace)
Also you can run diagnose to try to detect the root of the problem
[Run diagnose](command:sweetpad.build.diagnoseSetup)
No devicectl found. Install the latest version of Xcode to manage device.

No devices found. Use Xcode to pair a device:
Product -> Destination -> Manage Run destination.
[Open Xcode](command:sweetpad.build.openXcode)

commands.title: SweetPad: Set up Swift code intelligence (BSP)
SweetPad.Testing: Build for testing (without running tests)
SweetPad: Generate an Xcode project using XcodeGen
SweetPad: Generate an Xcode project using Tuist
SweetPad: Install Swift Package using Tuist
SweetPad: Edit Tuist project (Open project in Xcode)
SweetPad: Test Generated project using Tuist
SweetPad: Create Issue on GitHub (No Schemes)
SweetPad: Get app path for debugging

sweetpad.format.path.description: Command or path to formatter executable.

sweetpad.format.args.description: Command or path to formatter executable. Use ${file} as a placeholder for the file path. Placeholder ${file} is supported only as a separate item in the array.

sweetpad.format.selectionArgs.description: Custom arguments to pass to the formatter for selection formatting. Use ${startLine} and ${endLine} as placeholders for the start and end line numbers of the selected code. Use ${startOffset} and ${endOffset} as placeholders for the start and end offset of the selected code. If this is not provided for custom formatters, the entire document will always be reformatted.

sweetpad.build.xcodeWorkspacePath.description: Path to Xcode workspace. Can be absolute or relative to the workspace root.

sweetpad.build.xcodebuildCommand.description: Custom xcodebuild command or path. Supports environment variable expansion via ${env:VAR_NAME} syntax.

sweetpad.build.swiftCommand.description: Custom swift command or path. Supports environment variable expansion via ${env:VAR_NAME} syntax.

sweetpad.build.derivedDataPath.description: Path to derived data directory. Can be absolute or relative to the workspace root.

sweetpad.build.arch.description: Architecture to build for. Can be usefull for building x86_64 simulator builds on M1 Macs. Usually, you don't need to change this setting.

sweetpad.build.schemes.include.description: Only show schemes matching these patterns in the Build view. Supports `*` as a wildcard. Empty means show all.

sweetpad.build.schemes.exclude.description: Hide schemes matching these patterns in the Build view. Supports `*` as a wildcard. Applied after `include`.

sweetpad.build.allowProvisioningUpdates.description: Allow Xcode to update provisioning profiles.

sweetpad.build.args.description: Additional arguments to pass to the build command. You can also override the default arguments

sweetpad.build.env.description: Environment variables to pass to the xcodebuild command

sweetpad.build.launchArgs.description: Arguments to pass to the app on launch

sweetpad.build.launchEnv.description: Environment variables to pass to the app on launch

sweetpad.build.bringSimulatorToForegroun.description: Bring Simulator to foreground on launch

enumDescriptions: Child process + pipes. Use this if the v3 pty-based executor misbehaves.
node-pty executor: real pseudoterminal, colored output, TUI-friendly, shared shell env.
sweetpad.system.taskExecutor.description: Task executor used for SweetPad build/run/test/tool tasks.

sweetpad.shellEnv.timeout.description: Milliseconds to wait for the login shell to dump its environment. Increase if your shell startup files are slow.

sweetpad.shellEnv.shell.description: Override the shell used to resolve the environment (defaults to $SHELL). Example: /bin/zsh.

sweetpad.system.enableSentry.description: Enable Sentry error reporting. NOTE: This config is disable by default, I recommend enabling it when you have issues with SweetPad and want to report about them.

sweetpad.system.autoRevealTerminal.description: Automatically reveal the terminal when executing a command or task

sweetpad.system.showProgressStatusBar.description: Show progress in the status bar when executing a command or task

sweetpad.system.xcodebuildFallback.description: When the bundled resolver fails to read build settings from project files, retry the query via `xcodebuild -showBuildSettings` instead of failing. Useful as a safety net for exotic or malformed projects; slower than the bundled resolver.

sweetpad.xcodegen.autogenerate.description: Watch for new .swift files and regenerate the project using XcodeGen. Restart VSCode to apply the settings.

sweetpad.tuist.autogenerate.description: Watch for new .swift files and regenerate the project using Tuist. Restart VSCode to apply the settings.

sweetpad.tuist.generate.env.description: Environment variables to pass to the Tuist generate command — https://docs.tuist.dev/en/guides/develop/projects/dynamic-configuration

markdownEnumDescriptions: SweetPad's own BSP server, which derives compiler arguments from the project (no build required to parse). Experimental — currently applies only to plain `.xcodeproj` projects; `.xcworkspace`/SPM fall back to `xcode-build-server`.
sweetpad.buildServer.provider.description: Which build server backs sourcekit-lsp (writes buildServer.json).

sweetpad.buildServer.logPath.markdownDescription: Debug log file for the `sweetpad` BSP server. The server appends per-request state — resolved SDK/platform, compiler-argument counts, errors — there; `tail -f` it to see what sourcekit-lsp receives. Defaults to `.sweetpad/bsp.log`; set this to override the location (a relative path, or `${workspaceFolder}/…`, resolves against the workspace folder).

sweetpad.buildServer.logLevel.markdownDescription: Verbosity of the `sweetpad` BSP server's live log stream in the "SweetPad: BSP" output channel. The `.sweetpad/bsp.log` file always captures everything regardless of this setting.

sweetpad.xcodebuildserver.autogenerate.description: Watch if default scheme is changed and regenerate the build server config
sweetpad.xcodebuildserver.autogenerate.markdownDeprecationMessage: Use `#sweetpad.build.autoGenerateBuildServerConfig#` instead. This setting is still honored as a fallback when the new one is unset.

sweetpad.xcodebuildserver.serverEnv.description: Environment variables for the long-running xcode-build-server process that sourcekit-lsp spawns from `buildServer.json`. Injected by prefixing the generated `argv` with `/usr/bin/env KEY=VAL ...` (BSP has no env field).

sweetpad.build.autoRefreshSchemes.description: Automatically refresh schemes when project files change (Package.swift, .xcodeproj, .xcworkspace, etc.)

sweetpad.build.autoRefreshSchemesDelay.description: Delay in milliseconds before auto-refreshing schemes after detecting file changes

sweetpad.build.autoGenerateBuildServerCo.description: Automatically (re)generate `buildServer.json` via xcode-build-server on build and on scheme change. Disable when using a custom build server (e.g. one based on Swift Build) so SweetPad does not overwrite your config.

sweetpad.build.autoRestartSwiftLSP.description: Restart the Swift language server after every build and after `tuist generate` / `tuist install` / `xcodegen generate`. Disable when using a build server that does not require LSP restarts (e.g. one with background indexing). Has no effect on the explicit 'SweetPad: Generate Build Server Config' command, which always restarts.

sweetpad.build.logStreamEnabled.description: Enable os_log/Logger streaming in the task terminal during app launch. When enabled, os_log output appears alongside print() and build output in the same terminal.

sweetpad.build.logStreamPredicate.description: Custom predicate for log stream filtering. Use ${bundleId} and ${processName} as placeholders.

sweetpad.build.deviceTunnelAutoStart.description: Auto-start `pymobiledevice3 remote tunneld` (sudo) in a terminal before launching on a physical iOS device, if it isn't already running.

sweetpad.build.pymobiledevice3Path.description: Path to the pymobiledevice3 binary used to stream os_log/Logger output from physical iOS devices. Leave as 'pymobiledevice3' to resolve via PATH. Requires `pip install pymobiledevice3` and, on iOS 17+, a running tunnel (`sudo pymobiledevice3 remote tunneld`). Disable streaming entirely via build.logStreamEnabled.

sweetpad.build.pymobiledevice3ExtraArgs.description: Extra CLI arguments appended to 'pymobiledevice3 syslog live'.

sweetpad.build.pymobiledevice3SubsystemD.description: Glob patterns for subsystems to drop (e.g. 'com.apple.*').

sweetpad.build.pymobiledevice3SubsystemA.description: When non-empty, only keep entries whose subsystem matches a pattern (e.g. 'com.myapp.*').

sweetpad.cliServer.enabled.description: Run the in-extension JSON-RPC server over a Unix socket so the bundled `sweetpad` CLI (and AI agents) can read state and trigger builds from outside VS Code. See docs/dev/agent-cli.md.

sweetpad.hotReload.enabled.description: Launch the simulator (or macOS) build with InjectionNext pre-injected so file saves hot-reload into the running app. Requires InjectionNext.app — install it via the Tools view (opens the GitHub releases page; drag the app to /Applications). Adds `-Xlinker -interposable` and `EMIT_FRONTEND_COMMAND_LINES=YES` to the build, and sets `DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES` plus `INJECTION_PROJECT_ROOT` on the launched process. watchOS and physical devices are not supported.

sweetpad.hotReload.dylibPath.description: Override the InjectionNext `lib*Injection.dylib` path used for `DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES`. By default SweetPad resolves the right per-platform dylib inside `/Applications/InjectionNext.app/Contents/Resources/` (e.g. `libiphonesimulatorInjection.dylib` for iOS Simulator).
